neighborhoods.name: Le Marais (3e/4e)
neighborhoods.description: Historic quarter with medieval streets, Place des Vosges, Jewish heritage, and a thriving gallery scene
neighborhoods.character: Historic chic, walkable, cultural and gastronomic

housing.predominantType: Apartments in 17th-18th century Haussmann and pre-Haussmann buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Paris. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Boulogne-Billancourt
neighborhoods.description: Upscale western suburb with parks, museums, and good Metro access to central Paris
neighborhoods.character: Upscale suburban, parks, excellent Metro access

housing.predominantType: Modern apartments and Art Deco buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Paris's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Montreuil
neighborhoods.description: Eastern suburb with artistic community, flea markets, and lower rents with Metro Line 9 access

housing.predominantType: Apartments in older buildings and new developments

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Saint-Germain-des-Pres (6e)
neighborhoods.description: Left Bank literary quarter with famous cafes, bookshops, and a long history of international residents
neighborhoods.character: Literary, cafe culture, cosmopolitan Left Bank

housing.predominantType: Elegant Haussmann apartments and historic buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
